mnemonics.title: Kernel Image Rank
mnemonics.content: KIR: Kernel Is Range - No! KIN: Kernel Includes Null space, INverse relates to rank-nullity. Think: KIr = KIlling (kernel kills) vectors → zero output means kernel vectors.

mnemonics.title: Matrix Construction
mnemonics.content: COLUMNS = Transformed Basis Vectors. Take basis of domain, apply T to each, write results as columns in codomain basis.

mnemonics.title: Rank Nullity
mnemonics.content: RANK + NULLITY = TOTAL DIMENSION. Remember: RN = TD or 'Right Now Totally Different' to recall dimension adds up.

acronyms.usage: Set of vectors mapping to zero: ker(T) = {v : T(v) = 0}
Set of all outputs: im(T) = {T(v) : v ∈ V}
null(T) = dim(ker(T)) = n - rank for m×n matrix

visualTips.visualization: Think of matrix as a 'function machine': input vector enters from left, matrix transforms it, output vector exits from right. Each row gives one component of output.
Kernel shows which directions get squished to zero. In projection onto x-axis, y-direction collapses: all (0,y) go to (0,0).
Rank tells how many independent directions survive transformation. Full rank = no collapse; reduced rank = some squishing.

analogies.analogy: Like a manufacturing machine: takes raw materials (input vectors), applies fixed rules (matrix), produces products (output vectors). Same input always gives same output.
The 'discard' pile in a recycling plant - things that get thrown away (mapped to zero). The more in kernel, the more information is lost.
The number of independent 'paths' information can travel through. Higher rank = more independent outputs, less information compression.
